changes_summary: Addressed FLAG-001 by adding Step 3: wire the VideoGenerationModal's mode-switch and preset-select handlers through the provider (replacing raw `updateField` calls) so both surfaces use the atomic handler. Addressed FLAG-002 by specifying in Step 1 that the advanced-mode branch must clear `selectedPhasePresetId` to `null` when rebuilding phaseConfig on I2V/VACE toggle, since the rebuilt config won't match any stored preset. Also propagated user LoRA fields (lowNoisePath, isMultiStage) in the inline build calls.

success_criteria.criterion: handleGenerationTypeModeChange writes generationTypeMode, selectedPhasePresetId, and phaseConfig in a single updateFields call (no separate rebuildPhaseConfig call)
usePresetAutoSelect no longer fires on generationTypeMode changes — only on initial mount and shot navigation
User LoRAs (including multi-stage with lowNoisePath) are correctly merged into phaseConfig when switching I2V ↔ VACE
VideoGenerationModal mode-switch uses the provider's handleGenerationTypeModeChange, not raw updateField
VideoGenerationModal preset-select uses the provider's handlePhasePresetSelect (atomic updateFields), not two separate updateField calls
In advanced mode, switching I2V/VACE clears selectedPhasePresetId to null (stale preset ID cannot reach task payload)
All existing tests pass (npx vitest run)
handleRestoreDefaults also uses atomic updateFields instead of separate rebuildPhaseConfig call
rebuildPhaseConfig remains available for handleAmountOfMotionChange and handleSelectedLorasChange (incremental updates)
Manual smoke test: toggle I2V/VACE with LoRAs selected in both Shot Editor and VideoGenerationModal, verify phaseConfig includes user LoRAs and preset ID matches mode

assumptions: There is only one call site for usePresetAutoSelect (in useMotionControlPresetState). The modal's MotionControl also uses it via the same useMotionControlPresetState hook.
The updateFields method on shotSettingsRef batches all field updates into a single state write (no intermediate renders between fields).

The buildBasicModeGenerationRequest wrapper in modelPhase.ts (which divides motion by 100) is the correct function to use when building phaseConfig in the handler, matching current rebuildPhaseConfig behavior.

The VideoTravelSettingsProvider wrapping the modal shares the same shotId as the modal's own useShotSettings call, so provider handlers write to the same underlying settings — no conflict between the two.
Advanced mode users who toggle I2V/VACE should get a force-rebuilt phaseConfig AND have their selectedPhasePresetId cleared, since the rebuilt config won't match the previous preset.
